mnemonics.title: Digital Advantages
mnemonics.content: D.A.R.E. - Digital offers: Accuracy, Reliability, Flexibility, and Ease of reproduction

mnemonics.title: Logic Gates Types
mnemonics.content: A.O.N. - All OR Not: A (AND), O (OR), N (NOT). Other gates add variations: NAND, NOR, XOR, XNOR

mnemonics.title: Design Workflow Steps
mnemonics.content: S.F.L.C.P.T. - Specification, Functional design, Logic design, Circuit design, Physical layout, Fabrication, Testing

mnemonics.title: Binary Powers of 2
mnemonics.content: 1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 32, 64 - REMEMBER: Powers of 2 grow by doubling each time (2^0, 2^1, 2^2...)

mnemonics.title: Truth Table Rows
mnemonics.content: 2^n rows for n inputs - THINK: 'TO N' (2 TO THE POWER n)

acronyms.usage: Smallest unit of data in digital systems, can be 0 or 1
Language like Verilog or VHDL used to model digital circuits
Complete electronic circuit on a single semiconductor chip
Digital circuit family using bipolar junction transistors
Popular digital circuit family known for low power consumption
Technology for creating integrated circuits with thousands to billions of transistors

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a light switch array where each switch doubles the 'weight' - 1, 2, 4, 8... just like powers of 2
AND looks like D shape (all must be present), OR has curved input (any one is enough), NOT has bubble (inverts/includes circle)
Digital: staircase steps. Analog: smooth hill slope. Digital snaps to discrete levels, analog flows continuously
Think of truth table as a grid where each input doubles the rows: 1 input = 2 rows, 2 inputs = 4 rows, 3 inputs = 8 rows

analogies.analogy: Like counting with whole numbers (digital) versus measuring with a ruler showing every fraction (analog)
AND gate is like a series circuit - all switches must close for current. OR gate is like parallel switches - any one works
A bit is like a light switch (on/off). A byte (8 bits) is like 8 switches making a number - can represent 256 different patterns
Sequential circuits are like a recipe that uses previous steps' results. Combinational circuits are like a calculator showing only current input
HDL is like architectural blueprints that can be 'built' into actual silicon circuits through synthesis
